description: The engine-enforced FCRA pre-adverse waiting period. This is the vertical's defining mechanic: the gate is a CLOCK. The criteria carry earliestFinalActionAt (the not-before instant: the pre-adverse notice's issuance time T0 plus the employer's committedWaitDays); the performer submits finalActionAt (when the final adverse action is actually taken) as completion evidence. The rules engine settles FULFILLED only when finalActionAt >= earliestFinalActionAt (datetime:min-inclusive, on-or-after) and FAILED otherwise — so the engine REFUSES to open the final-adverse-action window before the wait elapses. This converts the Tyus v. USPS problem (a too-short window is an actionable FCRA claim) from a detective after-the-fact dispute into a preventive gate. | THE DEPLOYMENT CROSS-CHECK (mandatory, F-787 class): earliestFinalActionAt MUST be computed as the referenced ironvale-pre-adverse-notice-v1's SIGNED timestamp (T0) plus committedWaitDays. The engine compares two supplied datetimes; it cannot itself reach into the referenced record's signed time, so an orchestrator that sets earliestFinalActionAt too early would pass the engine while understating the wait. The e2e wait-interval-check asserts earliestFinalActionAt == preAdverse.signedAt + committedWaitDays and finalActionAt <= this record's own signed time (no future-dating) — the same 'engine band + on-chain cross-check' posture as the finance screening gate (screen-vs-gate). | TOLERANCE MUST BE 0 (grace seconds default): a positive grace on datetime:min-inclusive WIDENS THE FLOOR EARLIER, silently allowing a final action before the window elapses — re-introducing the very short-window violation this gate prevents. The type cannot forbid a record-creation tolerance param; the orchestrator must never set it. | On FULFILLED, open the ironvale-final-adverse-action-v1 (the human authorization) and set waitGateRef to THIS record id.

preAdverseRef.description: recordId of the ironvale-pre-adverse-notice-v1 that started the clock (T0).

committedWaitDays.description: The waiting period the employer committed to in the pre-adverse notice (operator policy, not statutory). Echoed here for the cross-check.

earliestFinalActionAt.description: The not-before instant = the pre-adverse notice's SIGNED timestamp (T0) + committedWaitDays. Cross-checked by the wait-interval-check against the referenced record's signed time; the engine gate refuses to settle unless the actual finalActionAt is on or after this.

finalActionAt.description: When the final adverse action is actually taken. Must be on or after earliestFinalActionAt (engine-enforced) and no later than this record's own signed time (cross-checked).
